addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

message.content: Unfortunately, 'rocket' is offline.  I do have rdp access to beast but we have never set up that windows computer according to our system.  Once upon a time, I started a handbook to document first time setup.  Can you search this repo for all existing 'windows' references because I think that will surface one or more procedures to help this setup.  I also want you to search for 'wsl' and 'cygwin' references to see if we have enough specifications to set up beast without having access to rocket, we may have not documented.  Finally scout all those terms and identify paces/heats related to this.  I don't want to set up beast now unless we know precisely what to do and are certain that access to rocket isn't necessary for this.

content.text:
I'll start where I am: you've got RDP to **beast** (a fresh, never-configured Windows box), **rocket** (your formal `bujn-winpc` host) is offline, and you want to know — *before* touching beast — whether our existing docs are sufficient to set it up, and critically whether any step secretly depends on rocket being reachable. That's a "read everything first, act never" task, which is exactly right. Let me sweep the repo.

Let me kick off parallel searches: the Windows handbook/setup procedures, the WSL+Cygwin transport specs and account anatomy, and a JJK scout for related paces/heats.

input.prompt:
I'm investigating whether this repo has enough documentation to set up a brand-new Windows host from scratch (first-time setup). Search VERY THOROUGHLY for all "windows" references, focusing on SETUP / ONBOARDING / FIRST-TIME-CONFIGURATION procedures and handbooks.

Specific places to investigate (but search beyond these too):
- The RBHW handbook family: Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhw0_cli.sh and rbhwb_base.sh — what Windows procedures do they surface? Read them.
- Tabtargets: tt/rbw-hw* (HandbookWindows), tt/rbw-HWdd (DockerDesktop), tt/rbw-HWdc (DockerContextDiscipline) — what do they install/configure?
- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md — read its structure/scope (ssh-to-Windows transport discipline)
- The node profile dir: rbmm_moorings/rbmn_nodes/bujn-winpc/ — list and read what's there (BURN profile for the formal Windows host)
- Any Memos/memo-*windows*.md files — list them all and summarize each one's topic
- Any references to "first time", "onboarding", "fresh", "from scratch" near windows content
- BUK garrison / caparison / invigilate Windows setup ceremonies

For each procedure/handbook you find, report: the file path, what setup step it covers, and whether it reads like a complete standalone procedure or a stub/incomplete draft. I especially want to know if there's a partially-started handbook for first-time Windows setup.

Report findings as a structured list grouped by: (1) Setup/onboarding procedures, (2) Reference/transport guides, (3) Node profiles & config, (4) Memos. Quote short relevant excerpts (file:line). Do not modify anything — read only.

I need to set up a brand-new Windows host ("beast", reachable only via RDP) to match our existing Windows host ("rocket" / bujn-winpc, which is currently OFFLINE). The key question: do our docs specify enough about the WSL and Cygwin setup that I could reproduce it on beast WITHOUT having access to rocket to copy from or reference live?

Search VERY THOROUGHLY for all "wsl" and "cygwin" references across the repo. For each meaningful one, determine whether it documents HOW TO SET UP that component (installation, configuration, account creation, ssh keys, Docker daemon) versus merely HOW TO USE an already-configured one (transport/invocation discipline).

Specific places to investigate (search beyond these too):
- Memos/memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md — read it fully; it's described as the consolidated access reference + live account state for the Windows host. Does it describe how accounts were CREATED, or just their current state?
- Any Memos/memo-*windows-transport*.md or memo-*windows*.md — read and summarize
- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md — does it cover setup or just transport?
- rbmm_moorings/rbmn_nodes/bujn-winpc/ and any rbmu_ user profiles — read the config; what accounts/users/keys does it presume already exist?
- BUK garrison ceremony files (search for "garrison", "caparison", "invigilate") — do these CREATE the wsl/cygwin accounts or assume them?
- ssh alias definitions (brad@rocket, cygwin@rocket, wsl@rocket) — where configured, and do they presume keys already installed on the Windows side?
- Docker daemon setup under WSL — is the install documented?

The crucial deliverable: a clear assessment of GAPS — what would I need to do on beast that is NOT documented anywhere, and would normally require looking at rocket. List documented-vs-undocumented setup steps explicitly. Quote short excerpts (file:line). Read only — do not modify anything.

input.command: echo "=== memos mentioning windows/wsl/cygwin ===" && ls Memos/ | grep -iE 'window|wsl|cygwin' ; echo "=== 'beast' references across repo ===" && grep -rIl --exclude-dir=.git -i 'beast' . 2>/dev/null | head -50

aiTitle: Search repo for Windows setup documentation

content.text:
Now let me compile my comprehensive findings:

## Findings Report: Windows First-Time Setup Documentation

Based on a thorough search of the repository, here is the documentation landscape for Windows host setup from scratch:

---

### **(1) SETUP/ONBOARDING PROCEDURES**

#### A. **Handbook Windows (Operator-Manual Scope)**
**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJHW-HandbookWindows.adoc` (177 lines)

**Status:** Complete, standalone first-time setup procedure

**Coverage:** This is the **primary first-time-setup document for brand-new Windows hosts**. It covers the operator-manual scope that must be done before any automation:
- Install OpenSSH Server (GUI or PowerShell)
- Set sshd to Automatic and start
- Open SSH port through Windows Firewall  
- Enable temporary password authentication in sshd_config
- Ensure admin user has known password
- Apply security-sensitive registry edits (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` and `PlatformAoAcOverride`)
- Install Tailscale
- Toggle Tailscale Run-Unattended
- Authenticate Tailscale to tailnet
- Configure netplwiz auto-login

**Postcondition guarantee** (line 158): OpenSSH reachable with password auth enabled; Tailscale running; auto-login configured; registry hardening applied.

---

#### B. **Caparison Windows (Automated Multi-Phase Ceremony)**
**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJCW-CaparisonWindows.adoc` (300+ lines)

**Status:** Complete formal ceremony specification

**Structure:** Three phases automating what Handbook leaves off:
- **Phase 1** (lines 57-221): Admin SSH key trust + sshd hardening
  - Derive and install admin pubkey
  - Probe key-auth availability (CDD pattern)
  - Lock down authorized_keys ACLs
  - Rewrite sshd_config with hardening directives
  - Validate sshd, restart service
  
- **Phase 2** (lines 223-240): Reconnect and verify post-restart
  - Re-establish key-only SSH
  - Verify sshd_config served correctly
  - Preflight check on registry/Tailscale
  
- **Phase 3** (lines 258-300+): Provision infrastructure
  - Stage WSL distribution (`rbtww-main`)
  - Disable sleep/hibernate via powercfg
  - Set Tailscale service to Automatic

**Preconditions:** Requires BUSJHW Handbook complete first (line 45-50).

---

#### C. **Garrison Windows WSL (Workload Provisioning)**
**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc` (200+ lines)

**Status:** Complete workload account setup ceremony

**Coverage:** Post-caparison: sets up workload identity across three namespaces (Windows user, WSL distro registration, Linux user). Includes:
- Assert admin posture (via Invigilate)
- Export admin's WSL as seed tarball
- Obliterate phases (release hives, destroy profiles, scrub SAM, filesystem sweep)
- Create new workload user
- Import workload's own WSL distro
- Provision workload authorized_keys
- Route SSH entrypoints (Cygwin/WSL/PowerShell)

---

#### D. **Invigilate Windows (Health Probes)**
**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJIW-InvigilateWindows.adoc` (6738 bytes)

**Status:** Complete precondition/invariant verification

**Purpose:** Validates operator-manual scope (Handbook facts) and automated scope (Caparison/Garrison invariants) throughout the ceremony chain.

---

### **(2) REFERENCE/TRANSPORT GUIDES**

#### A. **Windows Scripting Guide (WSG)**
**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md` (29,628 bytes)

**Status:** Complete transport discipline documentation

**Content:** Codifies shell-scripting discipline for ssh → Windows stack (cmd.exe → PowerShell / Cygwin / WSL):
- Core Philosophy section: fault domains at each layer
- Transport Stack layers (lines 33-49)
- Established Rules: WSp-101 through WSp-109 (PowerShell); WSs rules (shell/bash); WSt rules (transport)
- Capture-Decide-Dispatch Pattern
- Idempotency Exemplars (copy-pasteable patterns for file checks, distro membership, user existence, service state, registry)

**Scope:** Answers "how to safely script remote Windows operations" — load-bearing for all ceremony code.

---

#### B. **Memo Series on Windows Transport Mechanics**

**memo-20260508-windows-transport-experiments.md** (594 lines)
- Empirical probe matrix for OQ-1 through OQ-7 (open questions about wsl.exe argv substitution, Cygwin vs WSL behavior, exit codes, etc.)
- Resolution: `$varname` / `${varname}` substitution happens at wsl.exe layer; escape rule is `\$name` for w-letter (wsl), none needed for c-letter (Cygwin)
- Per-letter escape table (lines 310-314)

**memo-20260510-windows-transport-ps5-anti-rationalization.md** (140 lines)
- Documents failure modes when PS bodies imitating wrapper shape → PS-5 rule tightening
- Introduces Capture-Decide-Dispatch (CDD) pattern
- Three cycles: guarded `if`, error suppression, correct CDD shape

**memo-20260511-windows-transport-wrapper-postlude-synthesis.md** (154 lines)
- Explains why the canonical PowerShell wrapper has exactly three slots (prelude / body / postlude)
- Elimination argument: why each token is load-bearing (LASTEXITCODE init, lazy-formatter guard, exit-code propagation)
- Rationale for wrapper code vs caller-body constraints

---

### **(3) NODE PROFILES & CONFIG**

**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/rbmm_moorings/rbmn_nodes/bujn-winpc/burn.env`

**Status:** Minimal BURN profile for the formal test Windows host

**Content:**
```
BURN_HOST=rocket
BURN_PLATFORM=bunne_windows
```

This is the **canonicalized Windows node reference** used throughout ceremonies and tabtargets.

---

### **(4) MEMOS & INVESTIGATION NOTES**

**memo-20260412-windows-handbook-draft.md** (363 lines)
- **Status:** Draft/reference (contains RBWHAB, RBWHAR, RBWHAX, RBWHEW, RBWHEU, RBWHEC, RBWHDD, RBWHDW, RBWHDC sections)
- **Note** (header line 3-4): "Consolidated 2026-05-31 — promoted to the single best-memory reference"
- Contains eight micro-procedures (RBWHAB through RBWHDC) covering:
  - SSH access setup (OpenSSH install, key provisioning, entrypoints routing)
  - WSL environment creation (install, user setup, systemd)
  - Cygwin environment (POSIX userland, curl path shadowing)
  - Docker Desktop and context discipline
- Appears to be **source material for the formal handbook** but marked as consolidated memo (not the canonical procedure).

**memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md** (488 lines)
- **Status:** Detailed reference document for SSH account mechanics
- **Scope:** Windows SSH account chain (profile creation, AuthorizedKeysFile resolution, ACL strictmodes, home-path disagreements Cygwin vs Windows)
- **Coverage:** Current access state on rocket; worked examples (brad@rocket setup); substrate-routing variants (cygwin/wsl dual-mode)
- Documents five load-bearing findings (profile lookups, Match blocks, orphan profile demotion, Cygwin/Windows home split, ACL allowlists)

**memo-20260517-windows-substrate-landscape-for-theurge.md** (495 lines)
- **Status:** Comparative analysis, not prescriptive setup guide
- **Content:** Cygwin vs WSL substrate fidelity for Recipe Bottle's theurge binary; operational traps when driving WSL from SSH; rustup prerequisites on Cygwin
- **Implication:** Documents that WSL is the preferred substrate for full fidelity; Cygwin requires path-transmutation workarounds

**memo-20260511-windows-hive-cleanup-reboot-decision.md** (163 lines)
- **Status:** Design exploration, decision rationale
- **Content:** Why unconditional host reboot was chosen as the canonical Windows state-reset primitive for destructive cleanup (vs attempted handle-hunt/service-stop tiers)
- **For operators:** Explains reboot cost (~30-60s) is acceptable in garrison context; implications for shared-host deployments

**memo-20260603-windows-docker-desktop-bind-mount.md** (111 lines)
- **Status:** Technical finding, membrane documentation
- **Content:** Fixes for nested bind-mount creation on Windows Docker Desktop (NTFS source + WSL2 backend)
- **For new hosts:** Pre-create nested mountpoints in bind source; use native dockerd, not Desktop socket

**memo-20260603-cygwin-ifrit-first-run-conntrack.md** (reference only, not read in detail)

**memo-20260601-wsl-skirmish-first-pass.md** (reference only, not read in detail)

---

### **(5) HANDBOOK DISPLAY FUNCTIONS (Tabtargets)**

**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/tt/buw-hjw.HandbookJurisdictionWindows.sh`
**Status:** Launcher stub (generic launcher pattern)

**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/tt/rbw-HWdd.DockerDesktop.sh`
**Status:** Launcher stub; displays Docker Desktop installation section

**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/tt/rbw-HWdc.DockerContextDiscipline.sh`
**Status:** Launcher stub; displays Docker Context Discipline section

These are display entrypoints (tabtargets) that render handbook sections; the actual content is in the `rbhw0_cli.sh` / `rbhwb_base.sh` / `rbhwht_handbook_top.sh` / `rbhwdd_docker_desktop.sh` / `rbhwcd_docker_context_discipline.sh` sourced from `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`.

---

### **ASSESSMENT: First-Time Setup Completeness**

**YES — adequate documentation exists for first-time Windows setup from scratch:**

1. **Operator-manual phase** (pre-automation):
   - BUSJHW-HandbookWindows.adoc covers all prerequisite steps (OpenSSH, Firewall, Registry, Tailscale, netplwiz)
   - Detailed and complete; operator-friendly with GUI/PowerShell alternatives

2. **Automated ceremony phase**:
   - BUSJCW-CaparisonWindows.adoc covers multi-phase automation after handbook
   - BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc covers workload provisioning
   - BUSJIW-InvigilateWindows.adoc validates both phases throughout

3. **Transport/discipline foundation**:
   - WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md establishes safe shell-scripting practices for the Windows stack
   - Memo series documents empirical findings backing the rules

4. **Reference/investigation material**:
   - Memo-20260412 (handbook draft), memo-20260516 (account anatomy), memo-20260517 (substrate landscape) provide detailed technical reference
   - These are not primary setup procedures but essential for troubleshooting and understanding failure modes

**Partially-started/stub procedures:** None identified. The memo-20260412-windows-handbook-draft.md is labeled as "consolidated" and was promoted to a reference memo, not left as an incomplete draft.

---

### **Key Files Summary Table**

| File Path | Type | Completeness | Purpose |
|-----------|------|--------------|---------|
| BUSJHW-HandbookWindows.adoc | Procedure | Complete | Operator manual (first-time setup) |
| BUSJCW-CaparisonWindows.adoc | Ceremony | Complete | Automated admin setup (3 phases) |
| BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc | Ceremony | Complete | Workload provisioning |
| BUSJIW-InvigilateWindows.adoc | Verification | Complete | Health checks/preconditions |
| WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md | Reference | Complete | Transport discipline & rules |
| memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md | Reference | Complete | SSH account mechanics & gotchas |
| memo-20260517-windows-substrate-landscape-for-theurge.md | Analysis | Complete | Cygwin vs WSL fidelity comparison |
| memo-20260508-windows-transport-experiments.md | Reference | Complete | Empirical open-question resolutions |
| memo-20260511-windows-*.md (3 files) | Reference | Complete | Transport wrapper, cleanup, PS-rules |
| memo-20260412-windows-handbook-draft.md | Reference | Archived | Consolidated to memo; source for handbook |
| bujn-winpc/burn.env | Config | Minimal | Windows node BURN profile |

- Obliterate phases (release hives, destroy profiles, scrub SAM, filesystem sweep)
- Create new workload user
- Import workload's own WSL distro
- Provision workload authorized_keys
- Route SSH entrypoints (Cygwin/WSL/PowerShell)

## COMPREHENSIVE ASSESSMENT: WSL and Cygwin Setup Documentation

Based on a thorough search of the repository, here is the status of setup documentation for Windows WSL and Cygwin:

### DOCUMENTED vs UNDOCUMENTED SETUP STEPS

#### **FULLY DOCUMENTED — What You CAN Reproduce Without Rocket**

1. **Windows OpenSSH Server (RBWHAB)** ✓ *memo-20260412-windows-handbook-draft.md*
   - Install via `Add-WindowsCapability`
   - Firewall rule for port 22
   - Basic `sshd_config` hardening (PubkeyAuthentication, PasswordAuthentication no, PermitEmptyPasswords no)

2. **Admin SSH Key Trust Setup (BUSJCW-CaparisonWindows.adoc)** ✓
   - Complete multi-phase ceremony documented (lines 1-359)
   - Phase 1: Admin pubkey placement in `administrators_authorized_keys`, ACL lockdown
   - Phase 2: Key-only reconnect verification
   - Phase 3: WSL distribution staging, Tailscale, powercfg
   - All single-statement SSH commands per WSG WSp-105 discipline

3. **Cygwin Installation (RBWHEC)** ✓ *memo-20260412-windows-handbook-draft.md, lines 224-266*
   - Install Cygwin to `C:\cygwin64`
   - Required packages: bash, openssl, curl
   - PATH configuration (Cygwin curl must shadow Windows curl)
   - No further account setup documented beyond this

4. **WSL Feature Enablement & Distribution Setup (RBWHEW)** ✓ *memo-20260412-windows-handbook-draft.md, lines 135-189*
   - `wsl --install` (requires admin elevation)
   - Ubuntu installation
   - Rename/import as `rbtww-main` via `wsl --export` / `wsl --import`
   - systemd enablement in `/etc/wsl.conf`

5. **Headless SSH Accounts (cygwin, wsl, brad)** ✓ *memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md, lines 215-246*
   - Account creation: `net.exe user /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes`
   - Directory structure: `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\<name>/`
   - ACL lockdown patterns (icacls commands)
   - Match blocks in `sshd_config` with absolute-path `AuthorizedKeysFile`
   - Both Cygwin and WSL forced-command wrapper scripts provided (lines 303-385)
   - **Dual-mode operation** (one-shot + interactive) with `SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND` branching

6. **Workload Account Provisioning (Garrison Ceremonies)** ✓
   - **Garrison-Cygwin (BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc)**: 8 steps, all documented
   - **Garrison-WSL (BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc)**: 12 steps, all documented
   - Both specify one SSH-as-workload session per WSG rule WSp-105
   - WSL per-user distro registration: `wsl --import` flow with pre-created seed tarball (lines 114-121)

7. **Docker Daemon on WSL** ✓ *memo-20260517-windows-substrate-landscape-for-theurge.md, lines 397-423*
   - `apt-get install -y docker.io` (version 29.1.3 observed)
   - `systemctl enable --now docker`
   - systemd must be enabled in distro first
   - Native dockerd coexists cleanly with Docker Desktop (no socket conflict)

8. **Windows Scripting Transport (WSG)** ✓ *Tools/buk/vov_veiled/WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md*
   - Complete transport discipline for cmd.exe, PowerShell, Cygwin bash, WSL bash
   - Per-letter escape rules (c-letter: none; w-letter: `\$name`, `\${name}`)
   - Base64 encoding patterns for transit through cmd.exe
   - ACL/file-write discipline (StrictModes, CRLF vs LF)

9. **Docker Desktop + Cygwin Bind Mount Fix** ✓ *memo-20260603-windows-docker-desktop-bind-mount.md*
   - Nested mountpoint pre-creation in source (`.gitkeep`)
   - Path normalization via `zrbob_native_path_capture`

#### **PARTIALLY DOCUMENTED — What Requires Context from Rocket**

1. **Tailscale Service Setup** ⚠️ *RBWHAB / CaparisonWindows*
   - **Documented**: Service installation and "Run-Unattended" config (handbook implies, caparison assumes)
   - **Not documented**: Initial Tailscale auth flow (login URL, code entry, device approval)
   - **Required from rocket**: The initial authenticated state (device already registered on tailnet)

2. **Registry Edits for Sleep Prevention** ⚠️ *CaparisonWindows preflight, lines 245-255*
   - **Documented**: `powercfg` command execution and expected outcome
   - **Not documented**: How to pre-configure `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` and `PlatformAoAcOverride` registry keys
   - **Note**: The handbook says these are "operator-manual scope" but gives no install instructions

3. **Firewall Rules** ⚠️ *RBWHAB, lines 20-24*
   - **Documented**: Single `New-NetFirewallRule` for port 22
   - **Not documented**: Any other firewall exceptions needed for the workload or Docker
   - **Likely needed**: Rules for container networking (if using on-host Docker)

#### **NOT DOCUMENTED — Critical Gaps**

1. **Cygwin SSH Account Setup** ❌
   - No mention of how `cygwin@rocket`'s forced-command wrapper and authorized_keys were created
   - The wrapper script location (`C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\cygwin\runcmd.sh`) is referenced in the memo but its **creation** is not documented
   - **Assumption**: Exists on rocket; garrison-c would create it, but not shown for ad-hoc setup

2. **Cygwin HOME Directory Alignment** ❌
   - memo-20260516, lines 163-196, documents the **problem** (split HOME between Windows profile and `/home/<name>`)
   - The **fix** (setting `HOME=/home/<name>` in `.bash_profile`) is shown in the worked example (lines 240-246)
   - **Not documented**: Whether this is **automatic** on garrison-c or **manual**

3. **Docker Compose v2 Installation on WSL** ⚠️
   - Mentioned in memo-20260601, line 61: `apt install docker-compose-v2`
   - **Not documented**: Why v2 is needed, whether it's part of standard setup, or alternatives

4. **WSL Distro Bootstrap Seed Creation** ❌
   - **Documented in garrison-w**: How to **use** a pre-exported seed (step 3, `wsl --import`)
   - **Not documented**: How the initial Ubuntu-24.04 seed tarball is **created** on rocket
   - **Question**: Was it created on rocket with `wsl --export Ubuntu-24.04 c:\bujb-wsl\ubuntu.tar`? If so, that creation step is missing.

5. **Station Regime Files (burs.env, station-files/)** ❌
   - WSL gauntlet bring-up requires these files present at `../station-files/burs.env`
   - **Not documented**: How to create or populate these for a fresh host

6. **Rustup Installation on Cygwin** ⚠️
   - Documented in memo-20260517, lines 186-228, but **empirical gotchas only**
   - **Not documented**: Complete step-by-step for rustup install (though TMPDIR workaround is clear)

7. **Cygwin OSTYPE Export** ❌
   - memo-20260517, lines 114-142, notes that theurge requires `export OSTYPE=cygwin` in parent bash
   - **Not documented**: Where/how this should be set (shell profile? launcher?)
   - **Current state**: Zero grep hits for OSTYPE export in buk/rbk launchers

8. **bash.exe Absolute Path Resolution for Cygwin** ❌
   - memo-20260517, lines 144-175: Theurge's `Command::new("bash")` finds `C:\Windows\System32\bash.exe` (WSL stub) before Cygwin's
   - **Documented**: The problem and fix pattern (resolve Cygwin bash path at startup via `cygpath -w`)
   - **Not documented**: Whether this is already fixed in the codebase or remains out-of-scope

#### **SUMMARY TABLE: Setup Completeness**

| Component | How to Set Up? | Installation? | Configuration? | Validation? | Status |
|-----------|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Windows OpenSSH** | memo handbook | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **Admin SSH trust** | CaparisonWindows ceremony | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **sshd_config hardening** | CaparisonWindows + handbook | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **Cygwin (POSIX environment)** | handbook (RBWHEC) | Yes | Partial* | Yes | ⚠️ Mostly |
| **Cygwin SSH account** | memo example (brad) | Yes | Yes (forced-command) | Yes | ⚠️ Ad-hoc only |
| **Cygwin HOME alignment** | memo example (lines 240-246) | No | Yes | No explicit | ⚠️ Shown, not automated |
| **WSL distribution** | handbook (RBWHEW) | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **WSL headless account** | memo + garrison-w | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **WSL native dockerd** | memo (lines 397-423) | Yes (apt) | Yes (systemctl) | Implicit | ✓ Complete |
| **Tailscale service** | CaparisonWindows | Implicit** | Yes | Yes | ⚠️ **Auth missing** |
| **WSL seed tarball** | garrison-w step 3 | No init | No | No | ❌ **Missing** |
| **Station regime files** | None | No | No | No | ❌ **Missing** |
| **Rustup on Cygwin** | memo (gotchas) | Partial (TMPDIR workaround) | Partial | No | ⚠️ Incomplete |
| **Docker Compose v2** | memo footnote | Yes (apt) | No | No | ⚠️ Minimal |

*Cygwin PATH configuration (curl shadowing) is documented.
**Tailscale is assumed "already installed and Run-Unattended + first auth" per handbook precondition (line 48).

---

### **KEY GAPS FOR A FRESH HOST (beast)**

To set up beast WITHOUT access to rocket, you would need to add documentation for:

1. **Tailscale initial authentication** — The handbook assumes it's done; first-time setup on a brand-new host needs:
   - Tailscale download/install
   - `Run-Unattended` registry configuration
   - Initial login flow (open URL, enter code)
   - Device approval on the tailnet

2. **Ubuntu-24.04 seed tarball creation**:
   - Bring up Ubuntu on an admin context
   - Run `wsl --export Ubuntu-24.04 C:\bujb-wsl\ubuntu.tar`
   - **Document this as a caparison Phase 3 sub-step**, not a separate manual step

3. **Station configuration files** (burs.env, etc.):
   - Copy from an existing working host, OR
   - Document the required schema and example values

4. **Cygwin theurge runtime fixes** (OSTYPE export, bash path resolution):
   - Either fix these in the source (move OSTYPE export into launcher), OR
   - Document the workarounds clearly in the handbook

5. **WSL systemd check** — systemd must be enabled before docker.io install:
   - Add explicit check/requirement to the handbook

6. **Docker Compose v2 rationale** — Why not docker-compose (v1)?
   - Document the version requirement (it's in memo-20260601 but scattered)

---

### **QUOTES FROM KEY SECTIONS**

**On account creation (fully documented):**
- memo-20260516, lines 94-95: `net.exe user brad "" /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes`
- memo-20260516, lines 225-226: `icacls C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\brad\authorized_keys /inheritance:r ...`

**On forced-command for Cygwin (fully documented):**
- memo-20260516, lines 322-323: `command="C:\cygwin64\bin\bash /cygdrive/c/ProgramData/ssh/users/cygwin/runcmd.sh"`
- The wrapper script itself is shown at lines 306-312 with full content

**On WSL per-user distro (fully documented):**
- BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc, lines 264-271: Four sessions: import, useradd, privkey, shutdown
- Precondition (lines 67-75): Seed tarball pre-staged at `C:\bujb-wsl\rbtww-main`

**On Docker native on WSL (fully documented):**
- memo-20260517, line 422: `apt-get install -y docker.io` (29.1.3); `systemctl enable --now docker`

**On missing Tailscale setup:**
- CaparisonWindows, line 50: "Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth" (precondition, not action)
- Handbook: No mention of how to install or initially auth

**On missing seed creation:**
- CaparisonWindows, line 275: References seed at `C:\bujb-wsl\rbtww-main` (created by import)
- But upstream creation of ubuntu.tar is not documented

---

## CONCLUSION

The repo contains **excellent detailed documentation** for the assembly chain (garrison, caparison, forced-commands, WSG transport rules), but has **operational blind spots** at the bootstrap level. You can reproduce the full workload-provisioning flow on beast using the garrison and caparison specs, but you'll need to fill in:

1. Tailscale initial setup (best: copy from rocket or get offline docs)
2. Ubuntu seed creation (best: run the `wsl --export` step explicitly on beast once base WSL is up)
3. Station configuration (best: copy burs.env from rocket)
4. Runtime fixes for Cygwin theurge (documented as gotchas, needs testing on beast)

The SSH account structure, forced-command routing, and Docker setup are **fully reproducible** from the memos and specs alone.

toolUseResult.prompt:
I need to set up a brand-new Windows host ("beast", reachable only via RDP) to match our existing Windows host ("rocket" / bujn-winpc, which is currently OFFLINE). The key question: do our docs specify enough about the WSL and Cygwin setup that I could reproduce it on beast WITHOUT having access to rocket to copy from or reference live?

Search VERY THOROUGHLY for all "wsl" and "cygwin" references across the repo. For each meaningful one, determine whether it documents HOW TO SET UP that component (installation, configuration, account creation, ssh keys, Docker daemon) versus merely HOW TO USE an already-configured one (transport/invocation discipline).

Specific places to investigate (search beyond these too):
- Memos/memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md — read it fully; it's described as the consolidated access reference + live account state for the Windows host. Does it describe how accounts were CREATED, or just their current state?
- Any Memos/memo-*windows-transport*.md or memo-*windows*.md — read and summarize
- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md — does it cover setup or just transport?
- rbmm_moorings/rbmn_nodes/bujn-winpc/ and any rbmu_ user profiles — read the config; what accounts/users/keys does it presume already exist?
- BUK garrison ceremony files (search for "garrison", "caparison", "invigilate") — do these CREATE the wsl/cygwin accounts or assume them?
- ssh alias definitions (brad@rocket, cygwin@rocket, wsl@rocket) — where configured, and do they presume keys already installed on the Windows side?
- Docker daemon setup under WSL — is the install documented?

The crucial deliverable: a clear assessment of GAPS — what would I need to do on beast that is NOT documented anywhere, and would normally require looking at rocket. List documented-vs-undocumented setup steps explicitly. Quote short excerpts (file:line). Read only — do not modify anything.

   - **Documented**: Service installation and "Run-Unattended" config (handbook implies, caparison assumes)
   - **Not documented**: Initial Tailscale auth flow (login URL, code entry, device approval)
   - **Required from rocket**: The initial authenticated state (device already registered on tailnet)

2. **Registry Edits for Sleep Prevention** ⚠️ *CaparisonWindows preflight, lines 245-255*
   - **Documented**: `powercfg` command execution and expected outcome
   - **Not documented**: How to pre-configure `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` and `PlatformAoAcOverride` registry keys
   - **Note**: The handbook says these are "operator-manual scope" but gives no install instructions

3. **Firewall Rules** ⚠️ *RBWHAB, lines 20-24*
   - **Documented**: Single `New-NetFirewallRule` for port 22
   - **Not documented**: Any other firewall exceptions needed for the workload or Docker
   - **Likely needed**: Rules for container networking (if using on-host Docker)

#### **NOT DOCUMENTED — Critical Gaps**

1. **Cygwin SSH Account Setup** ❌
   - No mention of how `cygwin@rocket`'s forced-command wrapper and authorized_keys were created
   - The wrapper script location (`C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\cygwin\runcmd.sh`) is referenced in the memo but its **creation** is not documented
   - **Assumption**: Exists on rocket; garrison-c would create it, but not shown for ad-hoc setup

2. **Cygwin HOME Directory Alignment** ❌
   - memo-20260516, lines 163-196, documents the **problem** (split HOME between Windows profile and `/home/<name>`)
   - The **fix** (setting `HOME=/home/<name>` in `.bash_profile`) is shown in the worked example (lines 240-246)
   - **Not documented**: Whether this is **automatic** on garrison-c or **manual**

3. **Docker Compose v2 Installation on WSL** ⚠️
   - Mentioned in memo-20260601, line 61: `apt install docker-compose-v2`
   - **Not documented**: Why v2 is needed, whether it's part of standard setup, or alternatives

4. **WSL Distro Bootstrap Seed Creation** ❌
   - **Documented in garrison-w**: How to **use** a pre-exported seed (step 3, `wsl --import`)
   - **Not documented**: How the initial Ubuntu-24.04 seed tarball is **created** on rocket
   - **Question**: Was it created on rocket with `wsl --export Ubuntu-24.04 c:\bujb-wsl\ubuntu.tar`? If so, that creation step is missing.

5. **Station Regime Files (burs.env, station-files/)** ❌
   - WSL gauntlet bring-up requires these files present at `../station-files/burs.env`
   - **Not documented**: How to create or populate these for a fresh host

6. **Rustup Installation on Cygwin** ⚠️
   - Documented in memo-20260517, lines 186-228, but **empirical gotchas only**
   - **Not documented**: Complete step-by-step for rustup install (though TMPDIR workaround is clear)

7. **Cygwin OSTYPE Export** ❌
   - memo-20260517, lines 114-142, notes that theurge requires `export OSTYPE=cygwin` in parent bash
   - **Not documented**: Where/how this should be set (shell profile? launcher?)
   - **Current state**: Zero grep hits for OSTYPE export in buk/rbk launchers

8. **bash.exe Absolute Path Resolution for Cygwin** ❌
   - memo-20260517, lines 144-175: Theurge's `Command::new("bash")` finds `C:\Windows\System32\bash.exe` (WSL stub) before Cygwin's
   - **Documented**: The problem and fix pattern (resolve Cygwin bash path at startup via `cygpath -w`)
   - **Not documented**: Whether this is already fixed in the codebase or remains out-of-scope

#### **SUMMARY TABLE: Setup Completeness**

| Component | How to Set Up? | Installation? | Configuration? | Validation? | Status |
|-----------|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Windows OpenSSH** | memo handbook | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **Admin SSH trust** | CaparisonWindows ceremony | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **sshd_config hardening** | CaparisonWindows + handbook | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **Cygwin (POSIX environment)** | handbook (RBWHEC) | Yes | Partial* | Yes | ⚠️ Mostly |
| **Cygwin SSH account** | memo example (brad) | Yes | Yes (forced-command) | Yes | ⚠️ Ad-hoc only |
| **Cygwin HOME alignment** | memo example (lines 240-246) | No | Yes | No explicit | ⚠️ Shown, not automated |
| **WSL distribution** | handbook (RBWHEW) | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **WSL headless account** | memo + garrison-w | Yes | Yes | Yes | ✓ Complete |
| **WSL native dockerd** | memo (lines 397-423) | Yes (apt) | Yes (systemctl) | Implicit | ✓ Complete |
| **Tailscale service** | CaparisonWindows | Implicit** | Yes | Yes | ⚠️ **Auth missing** |
| **WSL seed tarball** | garrison-w step 3 | No init | No | No | ❌ **Missing** |
| **Station regime files** | None | No | No | No | ❌ **Missing** |
| **Rustup on Cygwin** | memo (gotchas) | Partial (TMPDIR workaround) | Partial | No | ⚠️ Incomplete |
| **Docker Compose v2** | memo footnote | Yes (apt) | No | No | ⚠️ Minimal |

*Cygwin PATH configuration (curl shadowing) is documented.
**Tailscale is assumed "already installed and Run-Unattended + first auth" per handbook precondition (line 48).

---

### **KEY GAPS FOR A FRESH HOST (beast)**

To set up beast WITHOUT access to rocket, you would need to add documentation for:

1. **Tailscale initial authentication** — The handbook assumes it's done; first-time setup on a brand-new host needs:
   - Tailscale download/install
   - `Run-Unattended` registry configuration
   - Initial login flow (open URL, enter code)
   - Device approval on the tailnet

2. **Ubuntu-24.04 seed tarball creation**:
   - Bring up Ubuntu on an admin context
   - Run `wsl --export Ubuntu-24.04 C:\bujb-wsl\ubuntu.tar`
   - **Document this as a caparison Phase 3 sub-step**, not a separate manual step

3. **Station configuration files** (burs.env, etc.):
   - Copy from an existing working host, OR
   - Document the required schema and example values

4. **Cygwin theurge runtime fixes** (OSTYPE export, bash path resolution):
   - Either fix these in the source (move OSTYPE export into launcher), OR
   - Document the workarounds clearly in the handbook

5. **WSL systemd check** — systemd must be enabled before docker.io install:
   - Add explicit check/requirement to the handbook

6. **Docker Compose v2 rationale** — Why not docker-compose (v1)?
   - Document the version requirement (it's in memo-20260601 but scattered)

---

### **QUOTES FROM KEY SECTIONS**

**On account creation (fully documented):**
- memo-20260516, lines 94-95: `net.exe user brad "" /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes`
- memo-20260516, lines 225-226: `icacls C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\brad\authorized_keys /inheritance:r ...`

**On forced-command for Cygwin (fully documented):**
- memo-20260516, lines 322-323: `command="C:\cygwin64\bin\bash /cygdrive/c/ProgramData/ssh/users/cygwin/runcmd.sh"`
- The wrapper script itself is shown at lines 306-312 with full content

**On WSL per-user distro (fully documented):**
- BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc, lines 264-271: Four sessions: import, useradd, privkey, shutdown
- Precondition (lines 67-75): Seed tarball pre-staged at `C:\bujb-wsl\rbtww-main`

**On Docker native on WSL (fully documented):**
- memo-20260517, line 422: `apt-get install -y docker.io` (29.1.3); `systemctl enable --now docker`

**On missing Tailscale setup:**
- CaparisonWindows, line 50: "Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth" (precondition, not action)
- Handbook: No mention of how to install or initially auth

**On missing seed creation:**
- CaparisonWindows, line 275: References seed at `C:\bujb-wsl\rbtww-main` (created by import)
- But upstream creation of ubuntu.tar is not documented

---

## CONCLUSION

The repo contains **excellent detailed documentation** for the assembly chain (garrison, caparison, forced-commands, WSG transport rules), but has **operational blind spots** at the bootstrap level. You can reproduce the full workload-provisioning flow on beast using the garrison and caparison specs, but you'll need to fill in:

1. Tailscale initial setup (best: copy from rocket or get offline docs)
2. Ubuntu seed creation (best: run the `wsl --export` step explicitly on beast once base WSL is up)
3. Station configuration (best: copy burs.env from rocket)
4. Runtime fixes for Cygwin theurge (documented as gotchas, needs testing on beast)

The SSH account structure, forced-command routing, and Docker setup are **fully reproducible** from the memos and specs alone.

The picture is consolidating fast. There's one dominant heat — **₣A- `rbk-30-win-windows-remote-control`** — with ~70 paces, and three still-open (`rough`) paces that look like exactly the live edge of this question:
- **₢A-ABG** (`windows-setup-first-time-debug`)
- **₢A-ABJ** (`revert-wsl-stage-dev-cache`)
- **₢A-ABK** (`windows-reboot-primitive-spec-coherence`)

Let me read those three dockets verbatim.

## Character
Interactive operator-driven iteration. Spec and code both in
motion; BUS*.adoc is working-draft, not load-bearing yet. No
bounded cycle count.

## Goal
First-time Windows setup converges end-to-end on bujn-winpc —
caparison-windows lands, garrison-w lands, workload reach
(jwk/jwc/jws) round-trips. Spec and code in mutual agreement
at close.

## Working posture
Spec edits, bash edits, and live ceremony runs interleave.
Heat-affiliated commits acceptable for spec-only design pivots
that outpace implementation; pace-affiliated when code+spec
land together. Cleanly-separable follow-ons (Cygwin path,
handbook recast, comment annotations, downstream practice)
get their own pace rather than accreting here.

## Done when
Operator declares Windows setup converged with spec and code
coherent against observed Windows OpenSSH behavior on
bujn-winpc.

content.text:
## Character
Architectural promotion of a debug-loop accelerator, not a revert.

## Goal
Promote the DEV CACHE shape in `zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl` to
formal architecture under the admin-no-WSL principle: admin produces
the seed tar on disk and never registers rbtww-main in admin's
HKCU\Lxss. Drop garrison-w's `[w-export-seed]` step (which only
existed to export admin's running rbtww-main; admin no longer has
one).

Concretely:
- Remove the DEV CACHE comment-block scaffolding; the shape becomes
  the canonical implementation.
- Decide and document the canonical cache path (currently
  `C:\rbtww-seed.tar`) and where caparison places it: rename if
  warranted, or formalize the current path with rationale.
- Caparison Phase 3 retains [3/6] install + [4/6] export to the
  cache path, and [6/6] unregister of the Ubuntu-24.04 seed; drops
  [5/6] import-into-admin and the tar-removal half of [6/6].
- Garrison-w drops `zbujb_garrison_w_export_seed` and its
  `[w-seed-cleanup]` companion; `[w-session-1/4]` imports directly
  from the canonical cache path.
- Operator must still drive a one-time cache pre-stage on hosts
  where caparison has never produced the tar (or caparison must
  produce it idempotently on first run).

## Done when
- Caparison-windows on a fresh bujn-winpc (or equivalent) lands the
  cache tar at the canonical path and does NOT register rbtww-main
  in admin's HKCU\Lxss (verify: admin's wsl.exe --list does not
  show rbtww-main).
- Garrison-w on the same host imports rbtww-main into workload's
  HKCU\Lxss from the cache tar, without going through any admin-
  side rbtww-main export.
- BUSJCW and BUSJGW specs reflect the admin-no-WSL shape (this
  intersects with ₢A-ABK's scope — coordinate at mount time).

content.text:
## Character
Spec coherence to match unconditional-reboot architecture
landed under ₢A-ABG, plus admin-no-WSL shape landing under ₢A-ABJ.

## Goal
BUS*.adoc specs reflect both the reboot-as-precondition shape and
the admin-no-WSL caparison/garrison restructure:
- BUSJGW gains the reboot step + simplified Phase 2; drops
  [w-export-seed] / [w-seed-cleanup] step descriptions.
- BUSJCW adds a cross-reference noting caparison's role as the
  reboot helper's enabling contract (sshd-at-boot, Tailscale
  autostart, authkeys persistence). Caparison Phase 3 spec
  reflects the admin-no-WSL shape (cache produced, no admin
  registration).
- BUSJCW (or BUSJI as appropriate) adds a paragraph explaining
  why the absolute-path AuthorizedKeysFile in the Match block is
  structurally required, not a Cygwin path organization choice:
  Windows OpenSSH's relative-path resolution via
  GetUserProfileDirectoryW fails on headless accounts (no
  ProfileList registry entry). Cite
  `Memos/memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md`
  §Chain step 3.
- WSG mints (or doesn't) a WSp item codifying reboot-as-canonical
  -Windows-state-reset for destructive cleanup. Decision recorded
  either way.
- All three documents cross-reference the design memo at
  `Memos/memo-20260511-windows-hive-cleanup-reboot-decision.md`,
  which captures the rejected alternatives (Tier 1 in-process
  unload, Tier 2 handle-hunt, Tier 3 UserProfileSvc cycle, Level 3
  offline NTUSER.DAT injection) and the rationale for the chosen
  primitive. Cross-references prevent the rationale from being
  re-discovered every time someone reads the helper.

## Done when
- BUSJGW step numbering and prose match landed bash.
- BUSJCW cross-references the reboot dependency + admin-no-WSL
  caparison shape.
- BUSJCW (or BUSJI) cites
  `Memos/memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md` for
  the GetUserProfileDirectoryW rationale behind the absolute-path
  Match block.
- WSG decision recorded (new WSp or "deemed not load-bearing as a
  separate principle").
- All three specs cite `Memos/memo-20260511-windows-hive-cleanup-
  reboot-decision.md` at the appropriate sections.
- Implementation commits under ₢A-ABG (reboot) and ₢A-ABJ (admin-
  no-WSL) cited in spec edits.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
7	Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
8	WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
9	integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
10	WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.
11	
12	## Current Concept
13	
14	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
15	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
16	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
17	Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
18	in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
19	
20	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
21	
22	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
23	  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
24	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
25	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
26	  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
27	  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
28	  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
29	  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
30	  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.
31	
32	**BUJB constant:**
33	
34	- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
35	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
36	  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.
37	
38	**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**
39	
40	- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
41	  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
42	  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
43	  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
44	  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
45	  admin-shell operations.
46	- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
47	  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
48	  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
49	  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
50	  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
51	  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
52	  precondition check.
53	- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
54	  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
55	  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
56	  authorized_keys.
57	
58	**Garrison-destructive model:**
59	
60	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
61	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
62	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
63	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
64	- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
65	  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.
66	
67	**Cross-cutting premises:**
68	
69	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
70	  it by reference, never duplicates.
71	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
72	  material; operator owns all key administration.
73	
74	**Tabtarget colophons (20 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
75	
76	Invigilate has no tabtarget — it is invoked only internally (caparison
77	post-completion, garrison precondition via bujp_preflight). The
78	`bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux}` functions exist; no standalone
79	operator invocation.
80	
81	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
82	- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
83	- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
84	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
85	- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
86	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
87	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
88	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
89	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
90	- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3
91	
92	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
93	
94	BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
95	implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
96	mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
97	privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
98	distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
99	hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
100	live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.
101	
102	**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**
103	
104	Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
105	admin posture, then post-completion verification.
106	
107	Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):
108	
109	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
110	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
111	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
112	   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
113	   process never sees the password.
114	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
115	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
116	   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
117	   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
118	3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
119	4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
120	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
121	5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
122	   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
123	   values.
124	6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
125	   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
126	7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
127	   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
128	   expected phase boundary).
129	
130	Phase 2 (key auth only):
131	
132	8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
133	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
134	   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
135	   a session.
136	9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
137	
138	Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):
139	
140	10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
141	    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
142	11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
143	12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
144	    (idempotent).
145	13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
146	    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
147	    wrong.
148	
149	**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**
150	
151	Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
152	ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
153	auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.
154	
155	**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**
156	
157	Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
158	systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
159	invigilate-linux post-check.
160	
161	**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**
162	
163	Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
164	fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
165	and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
166	posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
167	remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
168	garrison's precondition check.
169	
170	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**
171	
172	1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
173	   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
174	2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
175	   only (no password fallback).
176	3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
177	   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
178	   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
179	4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
180	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
181	5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
182	   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
183	6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
184	   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
185	   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
186	7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
187	
188	## Heat Sequence
189	
190	**Spec foundation:**
191	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
192	- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
193	  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
194	  caparison)
195	
196	**Implementation:**
197	- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
198	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
199	  superseded colophons + handbook entries
200	
201	**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
202	- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
203	  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
204	- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
205	  then macOS/Linux)
206	- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
207	  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)
208	
209	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
210	- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
211	
212	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
213	- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
214	- Garrison-WSL exercise
215	- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
216	- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)
217	
218	## Deferred (named for future return)
219	
220	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
221	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
222	  only.
223	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
224	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
225	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
226	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
227	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
228	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
229	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
230	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
231	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
232	- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
233	  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
234	  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
235	  caparison phase or sibling verb.
236	- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
237	  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
238	  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
239	  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
240	  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
241	  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
242	  a time, never both running concurrently).
243	- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
244	  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.
245	
246	## Standing Notes
247	
248	- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
249	  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
250	  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
251	  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
252	  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
253	  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
254	  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
255	  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
256	  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
257	  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
258	  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
259	- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
260	  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
261	  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
262	  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
263	  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
264	- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
265	  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
266	  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
267	  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
268	  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
269	  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
270	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
271	  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
272	  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
273	  privkey-at-rest protection.
274	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
275	  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
276	  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
277	  precondition.
278	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
279	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
280	  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
281	  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
282	- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
283	  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
284	  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
285	  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
286	  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
287	  daemon ever runs concurrently.
288	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
289	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
290	  and path construction.
291	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
292	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
293	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
294	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
295	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
296	  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
297	- **Mac Remote Login enabled is an operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN
298	  target** (including the operator's own machine). The first admin SSH
299	  must reach the host before caparison can do anything; only the operator
300	  can flip Remote Login on a fresh machine.
301	- **Linux nodes require `openssh-server` installed and `sshd` enabled** —
302	  operator-managed, not in caparison's scope.
303	- **Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is an operator-managed
304	  precondition** wherever garrison needs sudo elevation. On macOS,
305	  admin-group membership for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is also operator-
306	  managed (equivalent in posture to the ssh-copy-id prerequisite).
307	  Blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations; scoped
308	  is preferred for shared hosts.
309	- **Workload shell is `/bin/bash` on every platform** regardless of
310	  platform default. macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path —
311	  bash is the BUK execution substrate everywhere.
312	- **Headless-account anatomy memo gates mounting of remaining rough paces.**
313	  `Memos/memo-20260516-windows-headless-account-anatomy.md` captures the
314	  chain from `net user /add` through SSH-reachable (profile registration,
315	  GetUserProfileDirectoryW, Match-block-as-load-bearing, orphan
316	  `C:\Users\<name>` causing `.machinename` suffix, Cygwin split-HOME,
317	  StrictModes ACL allowlist). Required reading at mount-time for the
318	  rough paces that follow. Two specific items surfaced 2026-05-16 that
319	  do not have explicit paces yet: (a) `BUJB_command_w` drops workload
320	  stdout via UTF-16LE because `$env:WSL_UTF8=1` is not set in the
321	  forced-command directive — diagnose+repair fits naturally inside ABG's
322	  convergence scope; (b) garrison obliterate may need wildcard cleanup
323	  of `C:\Users\bujuw_user.*` orphans to prevent silent profile-suffix
324	  mints on the next cycle — fits naturally inside ABJ's obliterate
325	  reshaping. ABK and AAx carry explicit memo citations in their dockets.
